title: Actualización Crisis en Haití — 12 abr 2026
summary: Al menos 30 personas murieron en una estampida en la fortaleza Citadelle Laferrière, patrimonio UNESCO cerca de Milot, el 11–12 de abril, presuntamente provocada por gas lacrimógeno policial durante un evento turístico juvenil masivo; el sitio fue cerrado indefinidamente. El CEP haitiano suspendió indefinidamente el registro de votantes y candidatos el 8 de abril —amenazando la fecha electoral del 30 de agosto de 2026— tras la falta de un decreto electoral requerido. La ONU advirtió el 11 de abril que Haití está en 'caída libre' con el Plan de Respuesta Humanitaria 2026 financiado solo ~19.5% ($880M necesarios) ante los recortes de USAID. Nuevos eventos cubren el 8, 11 y 12 de abril; sitio de la estampida añadido al mapa; víctimas actualizadas; brecha de financiamiento del HRP actualizada a ~19.5% en economía; reclamo sobre elecciones actualizado para reflejar la suspensión.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: UN Warns Haiti in 'Freefall', Calls Urgent Global Action as HRP Only ~19.5% Funded.

title: Haiti Collapse Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: IOM's Emergency Tracking Tool 88.2 (Apr 7) confirmed 13,000+ people newly displaced from Gran Grif's March 28–30 Artibonite offensive, with four-in-five taking refuge with host families across 16 displacement sites. Guatemala completed its 75-troop GSF military police deployment on April 2, bringing total advance GSF personnel to ~195 as the force awaits a larger Chadian contingent by end of April. A CNN investigation published April 6 found children make up approximately 50% of Haiti's active gang members, raising serious humanitarian concerns about upcoming GSF combat operations. The IMF confirmed on April 6 that Haiti met all end-2025 Staff-Monitored Program targets — a rare positive signal — and Haitian authorities requested a program extension to June 2027. KPIs updated to reflect 1.46M+ displaced and GSF advance force of ~195; econ updated with IMF SMP compliance.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: Haiti CEP Postpones Voter Registration Indefinitely, Threatening August 2026 Election Timeline.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: IOM Documents 13,000+ Newly Displaced From Artibonite Gran Grif Attacks.

title: Haiti Collapse Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: The Gang Suppression Force's first Chadian advance contingent (~50 troops) arrived in Port-au-Prince on April 1, joined by Special Representative Jack Christofides, marking the operational beginning of the UN-backed mission with a broader mandate than its predecessor including independent arrest powers. Gran Grif and allied Kokorat San Ras gangs continued Artibonite offensives in the days after the Jean-Denis massacre, advancing toward Marchand Dessalines and distributing cash to residents as a control tactic. Amnesty International issued a formal condemnation on April 5, calling the March 30 massacre a 'widespread failure to protect' and calling for human rights safeguards in the GSF framework. KPIs updated to reflect GSF deployment of ~120 personnel; Marchand Dessalines added to map.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Amnesty International Condemns Jean-Denis Massacre as 'Widespread Failure to Protect'.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: The New York Times and syndicated outlets published a widely circulated analysis on April 4–5, 2026 documenting the gap between the pace of GSF deployment and the ongoing gang massacres on the ground.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: 75 Guatemalan Army military police officers arrived in Port-au-Prince on April 2, completing Guatemala's pledged deployment to the Gang Suppression Force (GSF).

title: Haiti Collapse Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Gran Grif gang launched a pre-dawn massacre on March 30 in Jean-Denis, Artibonite, killing at least 16 confirmed and up to 70 per rights groups (RNDDH), wounding ~30 and displacing nearly 6,000 — the second major Gran Grif atrocity in Artibonite following October 2024's Pont-Sondé massacre. The Jean-Denis attack site was added to the map and a new casualty entry recorded with evolving figures. The MSS effectiveness claim was updated to reflect PassBlue's March 30 analysis questioning whether the Gang Suppression Force will meet its 5,550-person target, given US funding complications and Chad's uncertain participation. Meta headline and daycount updated to reflect the massacre and current crisis state.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: Rights Groups Raise Jean-Denis Death Toll to 70; PassBlue Questions GSF Viability.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Gran Grif Gang Massacres Civilians in Jean-Denis, Artibonite; Up to 70 Killed.

title: Haiti Collapse Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: IOM confirmed more than 1.4 million people are internally displaced in Haiti — a 40% surge since end-2024 — as the Gang Suppression Force awaits formal deployment in early April with only ~70 Salvadoran advance personnel on the ground. New event files document the MSF Bel Air clinic suspension amid ongoing Port-au-Prince clashes, Viv Ansanm's announced plans to form a political party ahead of August 2026 elections, the Haitian police Q1 report of 43 gang members killed in 32 operations, US funding complications slowing GSF pre-deployment, and two journalists remaining in gang captivity. KPIs updated to reflect the OHCHR March 24 report (5,519 killed in 10 months) and the 6.4 million Haitians needing humanitarian assistance; casualties updated with 2025 data showing security forces responsible for 63% of deaths. Claims updated with a new entry on GSF human rights accountability.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Haitian Police Report 43 Gang Members Killed in 32 Anti-Gang Operations in Q1 2026.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: Amnesty International Warns UN Human Rights Council: Haiti Crisis Cannot Be Normalized. US Funding Complications Delay Full GSF Pre-deployment as Gang Violence Continues.

title: Haiti Collapse Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: A major UN OHCHR report released March 24 documented at least 5,519 people killed and 2,608 injured in Haiti between March 2025 and January 2026, with security forces responsible for more deaths than gangs themselves and 247 documented instances of police summary executions. The Kenya-led Multinational Security Support (MSS) mission formally ended around March 20 after a first contingent of 215 Kenyan officers withdrew on March 17, giving way to the new UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF), which is expected to field up to 5,500 personnel by April 2026. Gangs have expanded beyond Port-au-Prince into the Artibonite and Centre departments, leaving 6.4 million Haitians—more than half the population—in need of humanitarian assistance and 1.4 million internally displaced. In a rare political milestone, Haiti's Provisional Electoral Council published its approved party list on March 26 ahead of elections planned for August 30 and December 6, 2026—the first in a decade—after a record 320 parties registered.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Gangs Tighten Grip on Haiti; 6.4 Million Need Humanitarian Aid as Violence Spreads North.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: The UN Human Rights Office (OHCHR) released a major report on March 24, 2026 documenting that at least 5,519 people were killed and 2,608 injured in Haiti between March 1, 2025 and January 15, 2026. Ahead of the GSF's formal April deployment, CNN and multiple analysts published assessments questioning whether the new force can break the cycle of failed international interventions in Haiti.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: As of late March 2026, 18 nations had pledged personnel to the Gang Suppression Force (GSF) authorized under UN Security Council Resolution 2793. Viv Ansanm Gang Coalition Announces Plans to Form Political Party for August 2026 Elections.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: MSF Bel Air Clinic Suspends Operations Amid Intensified Gang Clashes. Haiti's Security Vacuum Deepens as MSS Ends and GSF Awaits Deployment.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: The Kenya-led Multinational Security Support (MSS) mission formally concluded operations in Haiti around March 20, 2026, as the new UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF) began its phased arrival.

title: Haiti Collapse Tracker Update — Tue Mar 17, 2026
summary: A first contingent of 215 Kenyan police officers departed Haiti on March 17, 2026, marking the start of the formal drawdown of the Multinational Security Support (MSS) mission.
